CGAL::Surface_mesh.name: CGAL::Surface_mesh
CGAL::Surface_mesh.brief: This class is a data structure that can be used as halfedge data structure or polyhedral surface.
CGAL::Surface_mesh.detailed: It is an alternative to the classes HalfedgeDS and Polyhedron_3 defined in the packages PkgHalfedgeDS and PkgPolyhedron. The main difference is that it is indexed based and not pointer based, and that the mechanism for adding information to vertices, halfedges, edges, and faces is much simpler and done at runtime and not at compile time. When elements are removed, they are only marked as removed, and a garbage collection function must be called to really remove them. P The type of the point property of a vertex. There is no requirement on P , besides being default constructible and assignable. In typical use cases it will be a 2D or 3D point type. Is model of MutableFaceGraph FaceListGraph Graph Concepts

members.name: Property_map
members.brief: Model of LvaluePropertyMap with I as key type and T as value type, where I is either a vertex, halfedge, edge, or face index type.

members.brief: adds a property map named name with value type T and default t for index type I .
members.detailed: Returns the property map together with a Boolean that is true if a new map was created. In case it already exists the existing map together with false is returned.

members.brief: Copy constructor: copies rhs to *this . Performs a deep copy of all properties.

members.name: add_vertex
members.brief: adds a new vertex, and resizes vertex properties if necessary.

members.name: add_vertex
members.brief: adds a new vertex, resizes vertex properties if necessary, and sets the point property to p .
members.detailed: Several vertices may have the same point property.

members.name: add_edge
members.brief: adds a new edge, and resizes edge and halfedge properties if necessary.

members.name: add_edge
members.brief: adds two opposite halfedges, and resizes edge and halfedge properties if necessary.
members.detailed: Sets the targets of the halfedge to the given vertices, but does not modify the halfedge associated to the vertices. The function does not check whether there is already an edge between the vertices. the halfedge with v1 as target

members.name: add_face
members.brief: adds a new face, and resizes face properties if necessary.

members.name: add_face
members.brief: if possible, adds a new face with vertices from a range with value type Vertex_index .
members.detailed: The function adds halfedges between successive vertices if they are not yet indicent to halfedges, or updates the connectivity of halfedges already in place. Resizes halfedge, edge, and face properties if necessary. the face index of the added face, or Surface_mesh::null_face() if the face could not be added.

members.name: add_face
members.brief: adds a new triangle connecting vertices v0 , v1 , v2 .
members.detailed: the face index of the added face, or Surface_mesh::null_face() if the face could not be added.

members.name: add_face
members.brief: adds a new quad connecting vertices v0 , v1 , v2 , v3 .
members.detailed: the face index of the added face, or Surface_mesh::null_face() if the face could not be added.
members.argsstring: (Vertex_index v0, Vertex_index v1, Vertex_index v2, Vertex_index v3)

members.name: remove_vertex
members.brief: removes vertex v from the halfedge data structure without adjusting anything.

members.name: remove_edge
members.brief: removes the two halfedges corresponding to e from the halfedge data structure without adjusting anything.

members.name: remove_face
members.brief: removes face f from the halfedge data structure without adjusting anything.

members.name: number_of_vertices
members.brief: returns the number of vertices in the mesh.

members.name: number_of_halfedges
members.brief: returns the number of halfedges in the mesh.

members.name: number_of_edges
members.brief: returns the number of edges in the mesh.

members.name: number_of_faces
members.brief: returns the number of faces in the mesh.

members.name: is_empty
members.brief: returns true iff the mesh is empty, i.e., has no vertices, halfedges and faces.

members.name: clear_without_removing_property_maps
members.brief: removes all vertices, halfedge, edges and faces. Collects garbage but keeps all property maps.

members.name: clear
members.brief: removes all vertices, halfedge, edges and faces.
members.detailed: Collects garbage and removes all property maps added by a call to add_property_map() for all simplex types. After calling this method, the object is the same as a newly constructed object. The additional property maps are also removed and must thus be re-added if needed.

members.name: reserve
members.brief: reserves space for vertices, halfedges, edges, faces, and their currently associated properties.

members.name: join
members.brief: copies the simplices from other , and copies values of properties that already exist under the same name in *this .
members.detailed: In case *this has a property that does not exist in other the copied simplices get the default value of the property.

members.name: collect_garbage
members.brief: really removes vertices, halfedges, edges, and faces which are marked removed.
members.detailed: has_garbage() By garbage collecting elements get new indices. In case you store indices in an auxiliary data structure or in a property these indices are potentially no longer referring to the right elements.

members.name: set_recycle_garbage
members.brief: controls the recycling or not of simplices previously marked as removed upon addition of new elements.
members.detailed: When set to true (default value), new elements are first picked in the garbage (if any) while if set to false only new elements are created.

members.name: halfedge

members.name: halfedge

members.name: is_border
members.brief: returns whether v is a border vertex.
members.detailed: Advanced With the default value for check_all_incident_halfedges the function iteratates over the incident halfedges. With check_all_incident_halfedges == false the function returns true , if the incident halfedge associated to vertex v is a border halfedge, or if the vertex is isolated. If the data contained in the Surface_mesh is not a 2-manifold, then this operation is not guaranteed to return the right result.

members.name: is_border
members.brief: returns whether h is a border halfege, that is if its incident face is sm.null_face() .

members.name: is_border
members.brief: returns whether e is a border edge, i.e., if any of its two halfedges is a border halfedge.

members.name: set_vertex_halfedge_to_border_halfedge
members.brief: iterates over the incident halfedges and sets the incident halfedge associated to vertex v to a border halfedge and returns true if it exists.

members.name: set_vertex_halfedge_to_border_halfedge
members.brief: applies set_vertex_halfedge_to_border_halfedge(Vertex_index) on all vertices around the face associated to h .

members.name: set_vertex_halfedge_to_border_halfedge
members.brief: applies set_vertex_halfedge_to_border_halfedge(Vertex_index) on all vertices of the surface mesh.

members.name: is_isolated
members.brief: returns whether v is isolated, i.e., incident to Surface_mesh::null_halfedge() .

members.name: null_vertex

members.name: null_edge
members.brief: returns Edge_index (std::numeric_limits< size_type >::max()) .

members.name: null_halfedge

members.name: null_face
members.brief: returns Face_index (std::numeric_limits< size_type >::max()) .

members.name: adjust_incoming_halfedge
members.brief: make sure that the incoming halfedge of vertex v is a border halfedge if v is a border vertex.

PkgSurface_mesh.name: PkgSurface_mesh
PkgSurface_mesh.detailed:
Mario Botsch, Daniel Sieger, Philipp Moeller, and Andreas Fabri The surface mesh class provided by this package is an implementation of the halfedge data structure allowing to represent polyhedral surfaces. It is an alternative to the packages PkgHalfedgeDS and PkgPolyhedron. The main differences are that it is indexed based and not pointer based, and that the mechanism for adding information to vertices, halfedges, edges, and faces is much simpler and can be used at runtime and not at compile time. Introduced in: CGAL 4.6 BibTeX: cgal:bsmf-sm-26a License: GPL Classified Reference Pages Classes CGAL::Surface_mesh

Draw a Surface Mesh CGAL::draw() I/O Functions I/O for OFF files I/O for PLY files read_3MF()

members.name: operator+=
members.brief: Inserts other into sm .
members.detailed: Shifts the indices of vertices of other by sm.number_of_vertices() + sm.number_of_removed_vertices() and analogously for halfedges, edges, and faces. Copies entries of all property maps which have the same name in sm and other . that is, property maps which are only in other are ignored. Also copies elements which are marked as removed, and concatenates the freelists of sm and other .

members.name: operator<<
members.brief: This operator calls CGAL::IO::write_OFF (std::ostream& os, const CGAL::Surface_mesh & sm) .

members.name: operator>>
members.brief: Extracts the surface mesh from an input stream in OFF and appends it to the surface mesh sm .
members.detailed: This operator calls CGAL::IO::read_OFF (std::istream& is, CGAL::Surface_mesh & sm) .

members.name: draw
members.brief: opens a new window and draws a surface mesh.
members.detailed: Parameters of the drawing are taken from the optional graphics scene options parameter. A call to this function blocks the execution of the program until the drawing window is closed. This function requires CGAL_Qt6 , and is only available if the macro CGAL_USE_BASIC_VIEWER is defined. Linking with the cmake target CGAL::CGAL_Basic_viewer will link with CGAL_Qt6 and add the definition CGAL_USE_BASIC_VIEWER . SM which must be an instantiation of a CGAL::Surface_mesh <...> . GSOptions a model of GraphicsSceneOptions concept. sm the surface mesh to draw. gso the graphics scene options parameter. Advanced The real declaration of this function template is: template<class K, class GSOptions> void CGAL::draw(const CGAL::Surface_mesh<K>& sm, const GSOptions& gso);

members.name: draw
members.brief: A shortcut to CGAL::draw(sm, Graphics_scene_options{}) .

members.name: add_to_graphics_scene
members.brief: adds the vertices, edges and faces of sm into the given graphic scene gs .
members.detailed: Parameters of the cells are taken from the optional graphics scene options parameter gso . Note that gs is not cleared before being filled (to enable to draw several data structures in the same basic viewer). SM which must be an instantiation of a CGAL::Surface_mesh <...> . GSOptions a model of GraphicsSceneOptions concept. sm the surface mesh to draw. gs the graphic scene to fill. gso the graphics scene options parameter. Advanced The real declaration of this function template is: template<class K, class GSOptions> void CGAL::add_to_graphics_scene(const CGAL::Surface_mesh<K>& sm, CGAL::Graphics_scene& gs, const GSOptions& gso);

members.name: add_to_graphics_scene
members.brief: A shortcut to CGAL::add_to_graphics_scene(sm, gs, Graphics_scene_options{}) .

members.name: read_OFF
members.brief: extracts the surface mesh from an input stream in the IOStreamOFF and appends it to the surface mesh sm .
members.detailed: This function reads points, as well as vertex normals, vertex and face colors, and texture vertex coordinates if those attributes are available in the input. These last four attributes are stored in internal property maps of sm named "v:normal", "v:color", "f:color", and "v:texcoord" , respectively, which will be created if they do not already exist. If property maps are passed through named parameters (see below), then they are used instead of the internal ones. Ignores comment lines which start with a hash, and lines with whitespace. The graph sm is not cleared, and the data from the stream is added. Point The type of the point property of a vertex. There is no requirement on P , besides being default constructible and assignable. In typical use cases it will be a 2D or 3D point type. NamedParameters a sequence of Named Parameters is the input stream sm the surface mesh to be constructed np optional Named Parameters described below Optional Named Parameters <button class="collapsible"> vertex_point_map </button> a property map associating points to the vertices of sm Type: a class model of WritablePropertyMap with Surface_mesh::Vertex_index as key type and Point as value type Default: boost::get(CGAL::vertex_point, g) <button class="collapsible"> vertex_normal_map </button> a property map associating normals to the vertices of sm Type: a class model of WritablePropertyMap with Surface_mesh::Vertex_index as key type and a 3D vector type issued from the same kernel as Point as value type Default: If this parameter is unused, vertex normals (if they exist) will be written in an internal property map called v:normal . <button class="collapsible"> vertex_color_map </button> a property map associating colors to the vertices of sm Type: a class model of WritablePropertyMap with Surface_mesh::Vertex_index as key type and CGAL::IO::Color as value type Default: If this parameter is unused, vertex colors (if they exist) will be written in an internal property map called v:color . <button class="collapsible"> vertex_texture_map </button> a property map associating textures to the vertices of sm Type: a class model of WritablePropertyMap with Surface_mesh::Vertex_index as key type and a 2D vector type issued from the same kernel as Point as value type Default: If this parameter is unused, vertex textures (if they exist) will be written in an internal property map called v:texcoords . <button class="collapsible"> face_color_map </button> a property map associating colors to the faces of sm Type: a class model of WritablePropertyMap with Surface_mesh::Face_index as key type and CGAL::IO::Color as value type Default: If this parameter is unused, face colors (if they exist) will be written in an internal property map called f:color . The data in the stream must represent a two-manifold. If this is not the case the failbit of is is set and the mesh cleared.

members.name: write_OFF
members.brief: writes the surface mesh sm in the output stream, using the IOStreamOFF.
members.detailed: This overload of write_OFF(std::ostream&, const Graph&) will also output the following property maps internal to the surface mesh, if they exist and if they are not already present in the named parameters: vertex normals (property map named "v:normal" in the surface mesh) vertex colors (property map named "v:color" in the surface mesh) vertex textures (property map named "v:texcoord" in the surface mesh) face colors (property map named "f:color" in the surface mesh) Point The type of the point property of a vertex. There is no requirement on P , besides being default constructible and assignable. In typical use cases it will be a 2D or 3D point type. NamedParameters a sequence of Named Parameters os the output stream sm the surface mesh to be output np optional Named Parameters described below Optional Named Parameters <button class="collapsible"> vertex_point_map </button> a property map associating points to the vertices of sm Type: a class model of ReadablePropertyMap with Surface_mesh::Vertex_index as key type and Point as value type Default: boost::get(CGAL::vertex_point, g) <button class="collapsible"> vertex_normal_map </button> a property map associating normals to the vertices of sm Type: a class model of ReadablePropertyMap with Surface_mesh::Vertex_index as key type and a 3D vector type issued from the same kernel as Point as value type Default: vertex normals will be output using the internal property map, if it exists. <button class="collapsible"> vertex_color_map </button> a property map associating colors to the vertices of sm Type: a class model of ReadablePropertyMap with Surface_mesh::Vertex_index as key type and CGAL::IO::Color as value type Default: vertex colors will be output using the internal property map, if it exists. <button class="collapsible"> vertex_texture_map </button> a property map associating textures to the vertices of sm Type: a class model of ReadablePropertyMap with Surface_mesh::Vertex_index as key type and a 2D point type issued from the same kernel as Point as value type Default: vertex textures will be output using the internal property map, if it exists. <button class="collapsible"> face_color_map </button> a property map associating colors to the faces of sm Type: a class model of ReadablePropertyMap with Surface_mesh::Face_index as key type and CGAL::IO::Color as value type Default: face colors will be output using the internal property map, if it exists. <button class="collapsible"> stream_precision </button> a parameter used to set the precision (i.e. how many digits are generated) of the output stream Type: int Default: the precision of the stream os true if writing was successful, false otherwise.

members.name: read_PLY
members.brief: extracts the surface mesh from an input stream in the IOStreamPLY and appends it to the surface mesh sm .
members.detailed: To read a binary file, the flag std::ios::binary flag must be set during the creation of the ifstream . the operator reads the vertex point property and the face vertex_index (or vertex_indices ) property; if three PLY properties nx , ny and nz with type float or double are found for vertices, a "v:normal" vertex property map is added; if three PLY properties red , green and blue with type uchar are found for vertices, a "v:color" vertex property map is added; if three PLY properties red , green and blue with type uchar are found for faces, a "f:color" face property map is added; if any other PLY property is found, a "[s]:[name]" property map is added, where [s] is v for vertex and f for face, and [name] is the name of the PLY property. Point The type of the point property of a vertex. There is no requirement on P , besides being default constructible and assignable. In typical use cases it will be a 2D or 3D point type. is the input stream sm the surface mesh to be constructed comments a string used to store the potential comments found in the PLY header. Each line starting by "comment " in the header is appended to the comments string (without the "comment " word). np optional Named Parameters described below Optional Named Parameters <button class="collapsible"> verbose </button> whether extra information is printed when an incident occurs during reading Type: Boolean Default: false The data in the stream must represent a two-manifold. If this is not the case the failbit of is is set and the mesh cleared. true if reading was successful, false otherwise.

members.name: write_PLY
members.brief: inserts the surface mesh in an output stream in the IOStreamPLY.
members.detailed: If found, internal property maps with names "v:normal", "v:color" and "f:color" are inserted in the stream. All other vertex and face properties with simple types are inserted in the stream. Edges are only inserted in the stream if they have at least one property with simple type: if they do, all edge properties with simple types are inserted in the stream. The halfedges follow the same behavior. To write to a binary file, the flag std::ios::binary must be set during the creation of the ofstream , and the IO::Mode of the stream must be set to BINARY . The PLY format does not support 64 bits integer types. Therefore, if a property of type std::int64_t or std::uint64_t is found, it is converted to std::int32_t or std::uint32_t respectively while writing. Point The type of the point property of a vertex. There is no requirement on P , besides being default constructible and assignable. In typical use cases it will be a 2D or 3D point type. NamedParameters a sequence of Named Parameters os the output stream sm the surface mesh to be output comments a string included line by line in the header of the PLY stream (each line will be precedeed by "comment ") np optional Named Parameters described below Optional Named Parameters <button class="collapsible"> stream_precision </button> a parameter used to set the precision (i.e. how many digits are generated) of the output stream Type: int Default: the precision of the stream os Extra: This parameter is only meaningful while using ASCII encoding. true if writing was successful, false otherwise.
members.argsstring: (std::ostream &os, const Surface_mesh< P > &sm, const std::string &comments, const NamedParameters &np=parameters::default_values())

members.name: read_3MF
members.brief: extracts the surface meshes from an input 3mf file and appends it to output .
members.detailed: Point The type of the point property of a vertex. There is no requirement on P , besides being default constructible and assignable. In typical use cases it will be a 2D or 3D point type. filename the path to the 3mf file output a std::vector containing the CGAL::Surface_mesh s that will be filled by this function true if reading was successful, false otherwise.
